articles.description: Arsenal have reached the Champions League final for the first time in 20 years but did they over-celebrate Tuesday's win over Atletico Madrid?
articles.title: Champions League: Did Arsenal over-celebrate semi-final win over Atletico Madrid?

articles.description: England fly-half Zoe Harrison says the introduction of smaller balls for this autumn's WXV Global Series is "the worst decision someone has ever made".
articles.title: Zoe Harrison: Red Roses fly-half says smaller balls for WXV is 'worst decision ever'

articles.description: Bryson DeChambeau says he will focus on growing his YouTube channel and only "play tournaments that want me" if LIV Golf does not survive.
articles.title: Bryson DeChambeau: American to focus on growing YouTube channel if LIV Golf fails

articles.description: Bayern Munich's attack of Harry Kane, Luis Diaz and Michael Olise has hit 100 goals this season. With the second leg of the Champions League semi-final with PSG coming up, BBC Sport compares the two front threes.
articles.title: Bayern Munich v PSG: are Harry Kane, Luis Diaz and Michael Olise the best front three?

articles.description: The moment of reckoning has arrived for Chelsea's BlueCo ownership, which is now the target of an increasingly angry fanbase.
articles.title: Chelsea owners BlueCo face moment of reckoning amid fan backlash

articles.description: All the latest from the news conferences for Nottingham Forest, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace ahead of the clubs' Europa League and Conference League semi-finals.
articles.title: Football live: Europa and Conference League news conferences

articles.description: British tennis players have struggled with injuries throughout the clay-court season - but why?
articles.title: Emma Raducanu, Jack Draper and Sonay Kartal - why are so many British tennis players injured?

articles.description: Britain's 'special relationship' with the United States may be under renewed scrutiny, but when it comes to football, it appears to be in fine health, writes Dan Roan.
articles.title: How the Football Association helped US Soccer build its new home

articles.description: Haiti's record scorer talks of his link to Scotland before facing them at the World Cup, and how he recently escaped Iran.
articles.title: Duckens Nazon: Haiti's record scorer on facing Scotland at World Cup and escaping Iran

articles.description: The UK government says it is in "discussions about supporting potential bids" to host the Olympics and Paralympics in the 2040s.
articles.title: Olympic Games bid: UK government discusses hosting Olympics in 2040s
